⚠️ Verify Your Account Details to Prevent Unauthorized Access

---

**Body:**

Dear [First Name],

We noticed unusual activity on your account and would like to ensure your account details are up to date. To protect your account, please verify your personal information by clicking the link below:

[Verify Your Account Details Now](https://secure-bank-login.com/verify)

This is an automated message. Please do not reply directly to this email. If you have any questions, contact our customer service team at support@bank.com or call us at 1-800-123-4567.

Thank you for banking with us.

Best regards,  
The [Bank Name] Team  
[Bank Logo]

P.S. If you did not request this verification, please contact us immediately to secure your account.

If you're concerned about phishing attempts, here are some tips to stay safe:

1. **Verify the sender's email address** – Legitimate banks will never ask for personal information via email.
2. **Check for spelling and grammar errors** – Many phishing emails have poor grammar or typos.
3. **Never click on suspicious links** – Hover over links to see where they lead before clicking.
4. **Contact the bank directly** – If you're unsure about an email, call the bank using the phone number on their official website.
5. **Report phishing attempts** – You can report phishing emails to the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) or your local cybersecurity authority.

If you're concerned about phishing attempts, here are some steps you can take to protect yourself and others:

1. **Verify the Source**: If you receive an unexpected email asking for personal or financial information, contact the organization directly using contact details from their official website or phone number.

2. **Check for Red Flags**: Be wary of urgent language, spelling errors, poor grammar, or suspicious links. Legitimate organizations will never ask for sensitive information via email.

3. **Use Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)**: Enable MFA on your accounts to add an extra layer of security.

4. **Report Phishing Attempts**: If you receive a phishing email, report it to the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) at [ReportFraud.ftc.gov](https://reportfraud.ftc.gov/) or to your local cybercrime unit.

5. **Educate Yourself and Others**: Stay informed about the latest phishing tactics and share this knowledge with friends, family, and colleagues.
